mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 850CSi engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned S70B56 V12 engine typically costs £10,000–£16,000 supply only — and that's if you can find one. Only 1,510 were ever built. Fitting adds £2,000–£4,000 depending on your location and workshop. A full specialist rebuild is often the most practical option for CSi owners. Main dealer new engines are no longer available. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

The S70B56 is a robust engine, but it's also a complex, 12-cylinder BMW from the 1990s. Common issues include DK throttle motor failure, MAF sensor and intake hose deterioration, and ignition system problems. With proper maintenance from a specialist, these engines can be very reliable — but they are not cheap to maintain. The engine itself is less of a concern than the age-related degradation of rubber and electronic components.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 850CSi?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most frequently reported issues are DK throttle motor failure (erratic idle, hesitation, EML light), MAF sensor failure (flat spots, poor throttle response), cracked rubber intake hoses (unmetered air, rough running), and ignition system failures (coils, distributor rotors, plugs). These are all age-related issues common to complex 1990s BMWs.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 850CSi engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: This depends entirely on the car's condition. A well-maintained 850CSi is now worth £60,000–£100,000+, making a £15,000 engine replacement a sound investment. Against a tired example needing other work, the maths changes. These cars are appreciating rapidly — only 1,510 were built worldwide, with just 160 right-hand drive examples — so a proper restoration is often justified. Get a specialist inspection before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 850CSi engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The 850CSi carries the engine code S70B56 (also referred to as S70 B56). It's a 5.6-litre V12 naturally aspirated engine, used exclusively in the E31 850CSi from 1992 to 1996. You can identify the S70B56 by the 'Powered by M' inscription on the central cam cover between the two banks of cylinders.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the DK throttle motors, or do they need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — DK throttle motor repair is a well-understood specialist job costing £800–£2,000. Rebuilding the existing motors is often more cost-effective than replacing them. If you experience erratic idle, hesitation, or EML warning lights, have the DK motors inspected immediately. Ignoring throttle motor issues can lead to more serious engine problems.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the S70B56 V12 engine. Only 1,510 S70B56 engines were ever built, making sourcing a replacement extremely difficult and expensive. A complete engine rebuild by a specialist is typically the most cost-effective option for a CSi owner. Prices above are estimates only — actual costs vary enormously depending on availability.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an 850CSi engine typically takes 20–30 hours at £80–£120/hr (independent specialist) or £150–£200/hr (London/SE dealer). The V12's complexity and the car's tight engine bay make this a labour-intensive job.

cta.label: → Compare 850CSi engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: DK Throttle Motor Failure
problems.symptoms: Erratic idle, hesitation on acceleration, flat spots in the throttle response, EML warning light, engine cutting out when returning to idle when hot.
problems.repairCost: £800–£2,000 (DK motor replacement or rebuild)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.replacementCost: £8,000–£18,000 (if left untreated and leads to engine damage)
problems.recommendation: The DK throttle motors are a known weak point on the S70B56. If you experience erratic idling or hesitation, have the throttle motors inspected. Replacement or rebuild is expensive but far cheaper than engine replacement. This is one of the most common issues on the 850CSi.

failureLink.label: Read the full throttle motor failure guide

problems.title: MAF Sensor & Intake Hose Deterioration
problems.symptoms: Flat spots in acceleration, erratic throttle response, lack of power, engine warning light, rough idle.
problems.repairCost: £300–£800 (MAF sensor replacement or cleaning; intake hose replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.recommendation: The rubber intake hoses between the airflow meters and throttle valves can crack with age, causing unmetered air to enter the engine. Dirty or failed MAF sensors can also cause flat spots. Regular inspection and replacement of ageing rubber components is essential on these cars.

failureLink.label: Read the full MAF sensor failure guide

problems.title: Ignition System Failures (Coils, Distributor Rotors, Plugs)
problems.symptoms: Engine coughing or skipping under acceleration, subtle shake at idle, misfires, loss of power.
problems.repairCost: £400–£1,200 (coil packs, distributor rotors, spark plugs)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.recommendation: The S70B56's 12-cylinder ignition system has 12 individual coils, two distributors, and 12 spark plugs. Over time, coils can fail, distributor rotors can corrode and seize, and plugs can wear. Replacing ignition components is a routine maintenance item on these cars — but with 12 cylinders, it's more expensive than on a six-cylinder. If you notice hesitation or misfires, have the ignition system inspected.

failureLink.label: Read the full ignition system failure guide

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 850CSi? Check These First
buyIf: * Full documented service history from a BMW V12 specialist
* DK throttle motors replaced or recently serviced
* MAF sensors and intake hoses inspected and replaced if necessary
* Ignition system (coils, distributors, plugs) serviced within the last 20,000 miles
* No EML or engine warning lights
avoidIf: * EML or engine warning lights with no documented diagnosis
* Erratic idle or hesitation with no DK throttle motor history
* Poor service history — the S70B56 is a complex engine that demands specialist care
* Evidence of overheating or cooling system issues

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 850CSi is a true BMW icon — the most powerful BMW up to that point, powered by a magnificent S70B56 V12, and produced in tiny numbers (only 1,510 worldwide, with just 160 right-hand drive examples). It's a future classic that's already appreciating rapidly. But it's also a complex, 12-cylinder BMW from the 1990s — and maintenance costs reflect that. DK throttle motors, MAF sensors, ignition components, and rubber hoses all need attention. Buy on documented history, not on faith — and budget for specialist maintenance. A well-maintained 850CSi is a £60,000–£100,000+ investment; a neglected one is a £15,000 repair bill waiting to happen.

cta.label: → Compare 850CSi engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 850CSi (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for smooth idle; any hesitation or erratic behaviour? (DK throttle motor warning)
* Service History — including the S70B56 era (1992–1996) and confirmed specialist maintenance
* EML Warning Light — Any history of EML or engine warning lights? (DK motor, MAF, or ignition issues)
* Throttle Response — Test acceleration; any flat spots or hesitation? (DK motor or MAF warning)
* Idle Quality — Any shaking, stumbling, or erratic idle? (DK motor or ignition system warning)
* Ignition System — Have coils, distributor rotors, and plugs been replaced recently?
* Intake Hoses — Have the rubber intake hoses been inspected or replaced? (ageing rubber is a common issue)
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
